image_prompt: SVG: four scatter-plot options A-D of Mass of sugar / g (y 0-600, ticks 100) vs Temperature / °C (x 0-120, ticks 20). Same six data points (0,190)(20,205)(40,245)(60,355)(80,365)(100,490). A and B join the points with straight segments (dot-to-dot); C draws a smooth concave-up best-fit curve through the points; D draws a straight best-fit line (intercept ~190, gradient ~3.0). Used to select the most appropriate line of best fit.

SVG: three stacked bar charts for three diets (Average US, Vegetarian, Vegan) with five food segments (Dairy, Fats/oils/sugars, Grains, Fruit & vegetables, Proteins). Panel 1 Carbon footprint / kg CO₂e person⁻¹ year⁻¹ (y 0-2000, ticks 500): US 2000 (Proteins ~1000), Vegetarian 1400, Vegan 700. Panel 2 Volume of H₂O / m³ person⁻¹ year⁻¹ (y 0-300, ticks 100): US ~295, Vegetarian ~90, Vegan ~70. Panel 3 Area of farmland / person⁻¹ year⁻¹ (y 0-4000, ticks 1000): US ~4000, Vegetarian ~1300, Vegan ~550. Shared colour key below.

topic: Beef vs cultured meat impact (bar chart)
image_prompt: SVG: grouped bar chart 'Environmental impact', Percentage / % (y 0-100, ticks 20) across four categories: Energy use, Greenhouse gas emissions, Land use, Water use. Beef = 100% in every category; Cultured meat = 55%, 9%, 3%, 6% respectively. Two bars per category with a Beef / Cultured meat key.

render_data.title: Build the CO₂ equilibrium equation
render_data.prompt: Select the symbols to form the equation showing the equilibrium between dissolved carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide gas above the drink:  ___  ⇌  ___

image_prompt: SVG: cut-through of a fizzy cola can/glass, dark drink with rising CO₂ bubbles, green side labels (own palette, not IB red). A downward arrow labelled CO₂ (gas dissolving) and an upward arrow labelled CO₂ (gas escaping) crossing the liquid surface, showing equilibrium between dissolved and gaseous carbon dioxide.

SVG: ball-and-stick 3D model of xylitol (C₅H₁₂O₅) — a five-carbon zig-zag backbone (black C atoms) each bearing an OH group (red O + grey/white H) and terminal CH₂OH groups, with C–H hydrogens. Key: C (black), O (red), H (grey/white). Title 'Xylitol'.

SVG: skeletal structural formula of aspartame — aspartic acid residue (HOOC–CH₂–CH(NH₂)–C(=O)–) joined by a peptide N–H bond to a phenylalanine methyl ester (–CH(CH₂C₆H₅)–C(=O)–OCH₃). Labels: O (three carbonyls), OH, NH₂, N–H, OCH₃, benzene ring. Blue line style on white.

SVG: two food cans side by side — a tin-plated steel can (silver body with a gold/bronze inner rim) labelled 'Tin-plated steel can', and a plain silver aluminium can labelled 'Aluminium can'. Both with horizontal ridges and subtle metallic shading.
SVG: a spiral notepad on a wooden desk with a pencil, showing handwritten raw data: 100°C–480.00 g, 0.00°C–200.00 g, 40°C–250.00 g, 60°C–365.00 g, 20°C–210.00 g, 80°C–370.00 g.

SVG: labelled cross-section of a candy-floss machine — central spinning container with holes (red sugar granules inside), spin arrows above, flames (Heat) beneath, molten sugar threads escaping sideways (red out-arrows) into a wide collection bowl, mounted on a motor base. Leader labels: Sugar, Collection bowl, Spinning container with holes, Heat, Melting sugar threads, Motor.

topic: Boiled eggs cut open by cooking time

stops.primary: Yolk: soft, just beginning to set
Yolk: pale, fully set with grey edge

image_prompt: SVG: dark egg tray holding ten halved boiled eggs in two rows, labelled by boiling time — top row 0,1,2,3,5 min; bottom row 7,9,11,13,15 min. Yolks progress from a flat runny orange yolk (0 min) through firmer set yolks to a pale, fully-set yolk with a greyish edge (15 min); whites set throughout.
SVG: concept map — boxes 'Sugars' and 'Proteins' with arrows converging into 'Maillard reaction (heat)', which then arrows out to 'New flavours' and 'Colour change'.

SVG: two slices of browned toast popping up from a pale toaster with a lever and ventilation slots; the toast shows Maillard browning speckles. Caption: 'Bread browned in a toaster (the Maillard reaction)'.

SVG: two clamp rigs with threaded posts and a digital force meter on the crossbar, each stretching a meat fibre clamped below — left meter reads 80 N, right reads 315 N. Labels 'Meat fibre'. Apparatus used to measure the force needed to break meat fibres in newtons.

SVG: three-panel cooking sequence for a lab meat-substitute (MY-P) cooked in citrus juice — Panel 1: 'Meat substitute strips' packet, pink raw strips and a yellow 'Lemon sachet'; Panel 2: timer 00:00, juice squeezed from a sachet onto pale raw strips on a plate; Panel 3: timer 15:00, cooked opaque orange strips on the plate.
SVG: two burgers side by side — 'Plant-based burger' with a lighter brown patty and 'Meat-based burger' with a dark patty and melted cheese; both with sesame buns, tomato and lettuce.

SVG: ruled data table comparing 'Plant-based burger (patty only)' vs 'Beef burger (patty only)'. Rows: Energy content/kJ 1000/1000; Total fat/g 14/13; Carbs/g 9/0; Protein/g 19/29; Fibre/g 3/0; Added sugar/g 'Less than 1'/0; Sodium/% recommended daily value 16/1; Vitamin B12/% recommended daily value 130/48. Shaded header row.

render_data.title: Lab-grown meat: sort each factor by perspective
render_data.prompt: Drag each fact about lab-grown meat into the perspective it belongs to, then evaluate its use from environmental, ethical, social, economic and health perspectives.
items: Fewer greenhouse gas emissions; large farms no longer needed
Cells taken from a live animal — no slaughter
Cost reduced to $10 per burger in 2020
80,000 burgers per tissue sample vs 1,250 per cow
Factory-farming jobs may be lost / re-training needed

image_prompt: SVG: interactive-style infographic of a black-and-white dairy cow with three labelled circular bubbles connected by lines — green 'Environmental factors', blue 'Social and economic factors', purple 'Ethical and health factors' — each with a small info (i) badge.
